name: Apollo 17
first: Last crewed mission to the Moon (and the only Apollo with a trained geologist)
description: The last time humans visited another world — over half a century ago. Cernan and Schmitt spent 75 hours on the surface, drove the LRV 35.7 km, and brought back 110.5 kg of samples. Schmitt was the only trained scientist (geologist) ever to walk on the Moon, and his findings — particularly the orange volcanic glass at Shorty Crater — reshaped lunar geology. Cernan's last words before climbing the ladder for the final time: 'We leave as we came, and God willing, we shall return.' Artemis III is supposed to make good on that promise.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Saturn V (AS-512) from KSC LC-39A 1972-12-07 — only Apollo night launch.

events.label: TOUCHDOWN
events.note: Lunar Module Challenger lands at Taurus-Littrow Valley 1972-12-11 19:54 UTC.

events.label: FIRST EVA
events.note: Cernan and Schmitt begin first of three lunar EVAs (total 22h 4m).

events.label: ASCENT
events.note: Cernan: 'We leave as we came, and God willing, we shall return.'

events.label: SPLASHDOWN
events.note: CM America returns crew to Pacific Ocean 1972-12-19.
